reasoning: “Yet” signals contrast between similar-positive adjectives; “and” is the natural coordination for delicious and satisfying.
Serta (and) joins two positives (menggugah selera, baik) per the patterns in 1–12.
Namun (but) appropriately contrasts a negative (hambar) with a positive (sehat).
Namun (but) is misused between two positives: baik and tidak berminyak (negation of a negative).
Serta (and) joins two negatives: tidak manis and berminyak.
Dan (and) joins two positives: manis and sehat.
Namun (but) contrasts sehat (positive) with tidak menggugah selera (negative).
Onde-onde (a cake) can be smooth and could be expensive; both plausible with the dictionary info.
Rempeyek is a fried, cracker-like food; calling it lembut (soft) contradicts its crunchy nature.
Kemplang (cracker) can be garing (crisp) and, when roasted, not oily (tidak berminyak).
Poffertjes are soft and sweet; describing them as garing (crunchy) and hambar (bland) is implausible.
